title: Robust Springy Greek Lamb Skewers
description: A quick and flavorful Greek dish featuring lamb skewers with a bitter and springy twist, incorporating Szechuan peppercorns and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, black pepper, and toasted Szechuan peppercorns.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto the soaked wooden skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
While the skewers are cooking, prepare a bain-marie by filling a large saucepan with water and bringing it to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and place a heatproof bowl over the saucepan, making sure that the bottom of the bowl is not touching the water.
Add the bay leaves to the bowl and let them steep for 5 minutes, or until fragrant.
Remove the skewers from the grill and place them on a plate, then brush them with the bay leaf-infused oil from the bain-marie.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh oregano and lemon wedges.
